LOCO Anomaly Inspector

Unsupervised Anomaly Detection with Frozen DINOv2
MVTec LOCO AD · 5 categories · No task training · Free-tier Colab T4 · Multi-seed evaluation

Our approach Pipeline

A multi-branch anomaly detection system built on frozen self-supervised DINOv2 patch features. No neural network training on anomaly data — only normal images are used.

Why DINOv2?

DINOv2 is a self-supervised Vision Transformer that learns rich visual representations without labels. Its patch tokens capture semantics, shapes, and spatial relationships — exactly what anomaly detection needs. We freeze the backbone and build lightweight scoring on top, making the system fast, reproducible, and deployable on free-tier hardware.

Detection branches

  • ■ PatchCore-style — k=1 nearest neighbour, top-1% aggregation (structural defects)
  • ■ Patch Memory — k=3 mean distance, top-5% aggregation (broader coverage)
  • ■ Region-Aware — spatially constrained patch comparison (layout-sensitive)
  • ■ Composition Histogram — bag-of-visual-words distribution (global composition)
  • ■ EfficientAD proxy — lightweight image-stat baseline (non-DINOv2)

The 4 flaws we found v1 → v2

We code-reviewed our own pipeline and found four design flaws. Each was fixed in v2, producing measurable gains. This honest self-improvement story is the centerpiece of our work.

Before & after Impact

The combined effect of fixing all four flaws. Every method improved — some dramatically.

Results leaderboard v2

All methods evaluated on the held-out test set. Thresholds derived from validation/good only — no test-label tuning. Multi-seed mean ± std across 3 seeds.

Key insights & limitations Honest

What we learned, what works, and where the approach falls short.

Comparison with published methods

MethodOverall AUROCApproachTraining?
SALAD (ICCV 2025)96.1%EfficientAD + SAM composition mapsYes (discriminative)
CSAD (BMVC 2024)95.3%Component segmentation + LGSTYes (student-teacher)
EfficientAD (WACV 2024)~90%PDN student-teacher + autoencoderYes (lightweight)
Ours — Fusion Max (v2)82.7%Frozen DINOv2 multi-branch fusionNo training
PatchCore (CVPR 2022)~82%WRN-101 memory bank + coresetNo training

Our approach is competitive with PatchCore while using a smaller backbone (DINOv2-small, 384-dim vs 1024-dim) and running entirely on free-tier Colab T4. Methods above 90% use task-specific training and heavier compute.

LOCO Anomaly Inspector — Data Analytics Lab, Spring 2026 · GitHub Repository · Built with frozen DINOv2 · No test-label tuning
